summary: DHH announces completing his full switch from Windows to Mac, using a 12-inch iBook as his sole computer for both work and personal use. The transition began in August 2002 but was delayed by the lack of adequate replacements for Windows-specific tools like Netcaptor (browser) and UltraEdit (code editor). With Chimera (later Firefox) reaching version 0.6 and Apple's Project Builder gaining PHP syntax highlighting, the final barriers to switching were eliminated.
key_points: DHH fully committed to Mac after a gradual multi-month transition from Windows
The main blockers were developer tooling: a capable browser and a code editor with PHP support
Chimera 0.6 and Project Builder with PHP highlighting removed the last obstacles
This early platform switch foreshadowed DHH's long association with Apple and Mac-based development
money_quotes: My computer joys of both pleasure and business now belong to a 12" Queen of Snow.
Without a serious contender to either Netcaptor nor UltraEdit, it just didn't seem feasible.
There were only apologies left. They be gone now.
key_insight: Developer platform loyalty ultimately hinges on tooling — once the essential tools exist, the switch becomes inevitable.
